Divine Attributes
beginner
5 min
The Quran is Uncreated: Affirmation and Prophetic Warning
Imam Samarqandi affirms the Quran's uncreatedness and cites prophetic warning.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand that the Quran is the uncreated speech of Allah, based on direct prophetic warning and the stance of early authorities.

Core Idea
The Quran is uncreated; those who say otherwise are disbelievers.

Imam Samarqandi begins with a clear declaration: the speech of Allah is uncreated. He then supports this with a narration from Sufyan al-Thawri and a hadith from the Prophet himself, which not only affirms the belief but also commands believers to avoid those who deny it. This lesson establishes the foundational position on the Quran's nature.

Guided Path
1
Read the opening declaration
Start by reading the first sentence: 'The speech of Allah is uncreated.' This is the core belief being defended.
Notice the direct and definitive tone.
2
Examine the narration from Sufyan al-Thawri
Sufyan al-Thawri states that whoever says the Quran is created is a disbeliever. This shows the gravity of the matter from an early authority.
3
Study the prophetic hadith
The Prophet foretold that a time would come when people would claim the Quran is created. He instructs believers to neither argue with nor sit with such people, as they are disbelievers who will not enter Paradise.
This hadith is a clear warning about the severity of the sin.
4
Reflect on Thabit al-Bunani's reaction
Thabit al-Bunani's response of kneeling in reverence upon hearing the hadith emphasizes the profound respect and seriousness due to this teaching.
It illustrates how the early Muslims received such traditions.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
كلام الله تعالى غير مخلوق وروي عن سفيان الثوري أنه قال من قال القرآن مخلوق فهو كافر بالله. أخبرنا الثقات بإسنادهم عن ابن عمر عن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم أنه قال سيأتي على أمتي زمان يقولون القرآن مخلوق فمن عاش منكم فأدركهم فلا تمارهم ولا تجالسهم فإنهم كفار بالله العظيم وإنهم لا يدخلون الجنة ولا يشمون رائحة الجنة. وقال ثابت البناني كنا إذا سمعنا هذا الحديث جثونا على الركبتين إجلالا لهذا الحديث. ومن وقف ولم يقل أنه كلام الله تعالى فهو شر ممن قال القرآن مخلوق. والواقف الذي يقول لا أدري القرآن مخلوق أم غير مخلوق ومثله كمثل النصارى الذين افترقوا على ثلاث فرق فقالت فرقة منهم إنا رأينا من عيسى إحياء الموتى وإحياء الموتى فعل الإله فنقول إنه إله. وقالت الفرقة الثانية منهم نحن رأينا منه العبودية فنقول إنه عبد. وقالت الفرقة الثالثة نحن رأينا منه الألوهية والعبودية فلا نقول إنه عبد ولا إله. والواقف يقول مثل هذا. اعلموا أن جميع ما أنزل الله تعالى من لدن آدم عليه السلام على أنبيائه إلى وقت محمد عليه السلام من الكتب مائة كتاب وأربعة كتب كلها كلام الله غير مخلوق. وروي في بعض الأخبار عن كعب الأحبار أنه قال أنزل الله تعالى أربعين صحيفة على شيث بن آدم وثلاثين صحيفة على إدريس وعشرين صحيفة على إبراهيم وعشر صحف على موسى قبل التوراة ثم أنزل التوراة على موسى والزبور على داود والإنجيل على عيسى والفرقان على محمد صلوات الله وسلامه عليهم أجمعين فجميع هذه الكتب كلام الله تعالى وصفته وهو غير مخلوق فمن قال كلمة منها مخلوق فهو كافر بالله يسمى جهميا ومعتزليا ولا شك في كفره فإنه مبتدع وهذه الحجة كفاية للعاقل
The speech of Allah, the Most High, is uncreated. And it is narrated from Sufyan al-Thawri that he said: Whoever says the Qur'an is created is a disbeliever in Allah. The trustworthy narrators informed us with their chain from Ibn Umar from the Prophet, peace be upon him, that he said: There will come upon my Ummah a time when they say the Qur'an is created. So whoever among you lives and reaches them, do not dispute with them nor sit with them, for they are disbelievers in Allah the Magnificent, and they will not enter Paradise nor smell its fragrance. And Thabit al-Bunani said: When we heard this hadith, we knelt on our knees out of reverence for this hadith. And whoever suspends judgment and does not say that it is the speech of Allah, the Most High, is worse than the one who says the Qur'an is created. And the suspender who says 'I do not know whether the Qur'an is created or uncreated' is like the Christians who split into three sects: one sect said, 'We have seen from Jesus the giving of life to the dead, and giving life to the dead is the act of the deity, so we say he is God.' The second sect said, 'We have seen from him servitude, so we say he is a servant.' The third sect said, 'We have seen from him divinity and servitude, so we do not say he is a servant nor God.' And the suspender says something similar. Know that all the books that Allah, the Most High, sent down from the time of Adam, peace be upon him, to His prophets until the time of Muhammad, peace be upon him, are one hundred and four books, all of them are the speech of Allah, uncreated. And it is narrated in some reports from Ka'b al-Ahbar that he said: Allah, the Most High, sent down forty pages to Seth son of Adam, thirty pages to Idris, twenty pages to Ibrahim, and ten pages to Musa before the Torah, then He sent down the Torah to Musa, the Psalms to Dawud, the Gospel to Isa, and the Criterion to Muhammad, may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon them all. So all these books are the speech of Allah, the Most High, and His attribute, and it is uncreated. Whoever says one word of them is created is a disbeliever in Allah, called a Jahmi and a Mu'tazili, and there is no doubt about his disbelief, for he is an innovator. And this proof suffices for the intelligent person.
Source pages p. 12

Commentary
This passage is a doctrinal argument establishing that the Quran (and all revealed books) is the uncreated speech of Allah, and refuting those who claim it is created. The author begins by stating the core belief: 'The speech of Allah is uncreated.' He then cites a narration from Sufyan al-Thawri that whoever says the Quran is created is a disbeliever. He then provides a hadith from Ibn Umar that the Prophet foretold a time when people will say the Quran is created, and commands believers to avoid them, as they are disbelievers. Thabit al-Bunani's reaction of kneeling in reverence shows the gravity of this issue. Next, the author discusses the 'waqif' (one who suspends judgment) who neither affirms nor denies. He ranks the waqif as worse than the one who says it is created, because the waqif's hesitation implies doubt. He then draws an analogy with the Christian sects who erred about Jesus: one said he is God, one said he is a servant, and one suspended judgment (neither). The waqif among Muslims is likened to that third Christian group. The author then informs that all 104 divine books (from Adam to Muhammad) are uncreated speech of Allah. He lists the specific books and pages revealed to various prophets. The conclusion is that whoever says any word of these books is created is a disbeliever, labeled as Jahmi or Mu'tazili, and is an innovator without doubt. The proof is sufficient for the intelligent.

Key Terms
كلام الله
غير مخلوق

Key Terms from Source Passage

Checkpoint
According to the hadith cited by Imam Samarqandi, what should believers do when they encounter those who say the Quran is created?
A) Engage them in debate to convince them.
B) Do not dispute with them nor sit with them.
C) Invite them to repentance.
D) Report them to the authorities.

Key Takeaway
The Quran is the uncreated speech of Allah, a belief affirmed by prophetic command and early scholars.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
If you were to meet someone today who denies that the Quran is uncreated, would you have the courage to avoid their company as the Prophet commanded? What would that cost you in terms of relationships or reputation?
?
The Prophet warned that a time would come when people say the Quran is created. Are we living in that time? How do you respond to those who question the Quran's eternal nature?
?
Thabit al-Bunani knelt in reverence upon hearing this hadith. When was the last time you physically expressed reverence for a truth of this magnitude? Will you commit to doing so today?

Divine Attributes
intermediate
5 min
The Waqif: Worse Than Open Denial
Imam Samarqandi condemns the waqif and uses a Christian analogy.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand why the one who suspends judgment (waqif) about the Quran's nature is considered worse than those who openly claim it is created, and the analogy used to illustrate this error.

Core Idea
Indecision about the Quran's uncreatedness is worse than outright denial.

Imam Samarqandi addresses a third group: those who neither affirm nor deny that the Quran is created. Surprisingly, he declares that this waqif is worse than the one who openly says it is created. He draws a powerful analogy with the Christian sects that split over Jesus's nature. This lesson reveals the danger of remaining neutral on essential matters of faith.

Guided Path
1
Read the definition of the waqif
The waqif is described as someone who says 'I do not know whether the Quran is created or uncreated.'
This is not a neutral position—it's a refusal to affirm the truth.
2
Understand the ranking
The author says the waqif is worse than the one who says the Quran is created. This emphasizes the necessity of affirming the uncreatedness.
Why? Because the waqif's hesitation implies doubt about what should be certain.
3
Examine the Christian analogy
Three Christian sects arose regarding Jesus: one said he is God, one said he is a servant, and one suspended judgment, saying neither. The waqif among Muslims is likened to that third, indecisive group.
The analogy shows that indecision is not a safe path but a form of error.
4
Draw the lesson
The waqif's position is condemned because it avoids the clear truth. True faith requires affirmation of the Quran as uncreated speech of Allah.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
كلام الله تعالى غير مخلوق وروي عن سفيان الثوري أنه قال من قال القرآن مخلوق فهو كافر بالله. أخبرنا الثقات بإسنادهم عن ابن عمر عن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم أنه قال سيأتي على أمتي زمان يقولون القرآن مخلوق فمن عاش منكم فأدركهم فلا تمارهم ولا تجالسهم فإنهم كفار بالله العظيم وإنهم لا يدخلون الجنة ولا يشمون رائحة الجنة. وقال ثابت البناني كنا إذا سمعنا هذا الحديث جثونا على الركبتين إجلالا لهذا الحديث. ومن وقف ولم يقل أنه كلام الله تعالى فهو شر ممن قال القرآن مخلوق. والواقف الذي يقول لا أدري القرآن مخلوق أم غير مخلوق ومثله كمثل النصارى الذين افترقوا على ثلاث فرق فقالت فرقة منهم إنا رأينا من عيسى إحياء الموتى وإحياء الموتى فعل الإله فنقول إنه إله. وقالت الفرقة الثانية منهم نحن رأينا منه العبودية فنقول إنه عبد. وقالت الفرقة الثالثة نحن رأينا منه الألوهية والعبودية فلا نقول إنه عبد ولا إله. والواقف يقول مثل هذا. اعلموا أن جميع ما أنزل الله تعالى من لدن آدم عليه السلام على أنبيائه إلى وقت محمد عليه السلام من الكتب مائة كتاب وأربعة كتب كلها كلام الله غير مخلوق. وروي في بعض الأخبار عن كعب الأحبار أنه قال أنزل الله تعالى أربعين صحيفة على شيث بن آدم وثلاثين صحيفة على إدريس وعشرين صحيفة على إبراهيم وعشر صحف على موسى قبل التوراة ثم أنزل التوراة على موسى والزبور على داود والإنجيل على عيسى والفرقان على محمد صلوات الله وسلامه عليهم أجمعين فجميع هذه الكتب كلام الله تعالى وصفته وهو غير مخلوق فمن قال كلمة منها مخلوق فهو كافر بالله يسمى جهميا ومعتزليا ولا شك في كفره فإنه مبتدع وهذه الحجة كفاية للعاقل
The speech of Allah, the Most High, is uncreated. And it is narrated from Sufyan al-Thawri that he said: Whoever says the Qur'an is created is a disbeliever in Allah. The trustworthy narrators informed us with their chain from Ibn Umar from the Prophet, peace be upon him, that he said: There will come upon my Ummah a time when they say the Qur'an is created. So whoever among you lives and reaches them, do not dispute with them nor sit with them, for they are disbelievers in Allah the Magnificent, and they will not enter Paradise nor smell its fragrance. And Thabit al-Bunani said: When we heard this hadith, we knelt on our knees out of reverence for this hadith. And whoever suspends judgment and does not say that it is the speech of Allah, the Most High, is worse than the one who says the Qur'an is created. And the suspender who says 'I do not know whether the Qur'an is created or uncreated' is like the Christians who split into three sects: one sect said, 'We have seen from Jesus the giving of life to the dead, and giving life to the dead is the act of the deity, so we say he is God.' The second sect said, 'We have seen from him servitude, so we say he is a servant.' The third sect said, 'We have seen from him divinity and servitude, so we do not say he is a servant nor God.' And the suspender says something similar. Know that all the books that Allah, the Most High, sent down from the time of Adam, peace be upon him, to His prophets until the time of Muhammad, peace be upon him, are one hundred and four books, all of them are the speech of Allah, uncreated. And it is narrated in some reports from Ka'b al-Ahbar that he said: Allah, the Most High, sent down forty pages to Seth son of Adam, thirty pages to Idris, twenty pages to Ibrahim, and ten pages to Musa before the Torah, then He sent down the Torah to Musa, the Psalms to Dawud, the Gospel to Isa, and the Criterion to Muhammad, may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon them all. So all these books are the speech of Allah, the Most High, and His attribute, and it is uncreated. Whoever says one word of them is created is a disbeliever in Allah, called a Jahmi and a Mu'tazili, and there is no doubt about his disbelief, for he is an innovator. And this proof suffices for the intelligent person.
Source pages p. 12

Commentary
This passage is a doctrinal argument establishing that the Quran (and all revealed books) is the uncreated speech of Allah, and refuting those who claim it is created. The author begins by stating the core belief: 'The speech of Allah is uncreated.' He then cites a narration from Sufyan al-Thawri that whoever says the Quran is created is a disbeliever. He then provides a hadith from Ibn Umar that the Prophet foretold a time when people will say the Quran is created, and commands believers to avoid them, as they are disbelievers. Thabit al-Bunani's reaction of kneeling in reverence shows the gravity of this issue. Next, the author discusses the 'waqif' (one who suspends judgment) who neither affirms nor denies. He ranks the waqif as worse than the one who says it is created, because the waqif's hesitation implies doubt. He then draws an analogy with the Christian sects who erred about Jesus: one said he is God, one said he is a servant, and one suspended judgment (neither). The waqif among Muslims is likened to that third Christian group. The author then informs that all 104 divine books (from Adam to Muhammad) are uncreated speech of Allah. He lists the specific books and pages revealed to various prophets. The conclusion is that whoever says any word of these books is created is a disbeliever, labeled as Jahmi or Mu'tazili, and is an innovator without doubt. The proof is sufficient for the intelligent.

Key Terms
واقف

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some think that suspending judgment is a safe, neutral position that avoids extremism.
Correction: The author explicitly states that the waqif is worse than the one who openly denies, because indecision about a clear truth is itself a grave error.

Checkpoint
According to Imam Samarqandi, to which Christian group is the waqif compared?
A) Those who say Jesus is God.
B) Those who say Jesus is a servant.
C) Those who say Jesus is neither God nor a servant.
D) Those who say Jesus is both God and servant.

Key Takeaway
Suspending judgment about the Quran's uncreatedness is worse than outright denial, as shown by the Christian analogy.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
When you are faced with a clear truth about Allah, do you ever hesitate to affirm it to avoid conflict? In that moment, you are worse than the one who openly denies it. Will you repent and speak the truth now?
?
Do you have any area in your faith where you say 'I don't know' when you actually know the truth but fear the consequences? What is the cost of your silence?
?
The waqif is like the Christian suspender. Examine your heart: have you ever created a 'third way' to avoid commitment? Ask Allah to give you the courage to affirm His attributes without reservation.

Divine Attributes
beginner
5 min
All Divine Books are Uncreated Speech
Imam Samarqandi enumerates all divine books and declares them uncreated.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand that all revealed scriptures, from Adam to Muhammad (peace be upon them), are the uncreated speech of Allah, and that calling any part of them created is disbelief.

Core Idea
Every revealed book is Allah's uncreated speech; to claim otherwise is kufr.

Imam Samarqandi broadens the scope from the Quran to all divine revelations. He lists the one hundred and four books sent down from Adam to Muhammad, specifying the pages given to earlier prophets. The lesson concludes with a definitive ruling: whoever says any word of these scriptures is created is a disbeliever, labeled as Jahmi or Mu'tazili. This comprehensive statement leaves no room for doubt.

Guided Path
1
Read the general statement
All books sent down from Adam to Muhammad are 104 in total, all of them the uncreated speech of Allah.
This includes all scriptures, not just the Quran.
2
Examine the list from Ka'b al-Ahbar
Ka'b al-Ahbar provides a detailed breakdown: pages to Seth, Idris, Ibrahim, Musa, and the books: Torah, Psalms, Gospel, and the Criterion (Quran).
This list shows the continuity of revelation.
3
Understand the conclusion
The author declares that whoever says any part of these books is created is a disbeliever, called a Jahmi or Mu'tazili, an innovator.
This is a strong condemnation of the created-Quran doctrine.
4
Reflect on the sufficiency of the proof
The author ends by saying this proof suffices for the intelligent person.
It emphasizes the clarity and strength of the argument.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
كلام الله تعالى غير مخلوق وروي عن سفيان الثوري أنه قال من قال القرآن مخلوق فهو كافر بالله. أخبرنا الثقات بإسنادهم عن ابن عمر عن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم أنه قال سيأتي على أمتي زمان يقولون القرآن مخلوق فمن عاش منكم فأدركهم فلا تمارهم ولا تجالسهم فإنهم كفار بالله العظيم وإنهم لا يدخلون الجنة ولا يشمون رائحة الجنة. وقال ثابت البناني كنا إذا سمعنا هذا الحديث جثونا على الركبتين إجلالا لهذا الحديث. ومن وقف ولم يقل أنه كلام الله تعالى فهو شر ممن قال القرآن مخلوق. والواقف الذي يقول لا أدري القرآن مخلوق أم غير مخلوق ومثله كمثل النصارى الذين افترقوا على ثلاث فرق فقالت فرقة منهم إنا رأينا من عيسى إحياء الموتى وإحياء الموتى فعل الإله فنقول إنه إله. وقالت الفرقة الثانية منهم نحن رأينا منه العبودية فنقول إنه عبد. وقالت الفرقة الثالثة نحن رأينا منه الألوهية والعبودية فلا نقول إنه عبد ولا إله. والواقف يقول مثل هذا. اعلموا أن جميع ما أنزل الله تعالى من لدن آدم عليه السلام على أنبيائه إلى وقت محمد عليه السلام من الكتب مائة كتاب وأربعة كتب كلها كلام الله غير مخلوق. وروي في بعض الأخبار عن كعب الأحبار أنه قال أنزل الله تعالى أربعين صحيفة على شيث بن آدم وثلاثين صحيفة على إدريس وعشرين صحيفة على إبراهيم وعشر صحف على موسى قبل التوراة ثم أنزل التوراة على موسى والزبور على داود والإنجيل على عيسى والفرقان على محمد صلوات الله وسلامه عليهم أجمعين فجميع هذه الكتب كلام الله تعالى وصفته وهو غير مخلوق فمن قال كلمة منها مخلوق فهو كافر بالله يسمى جهميا ومعتزليا ولا شك في كفره فإنه مبتدع وهذه الحجة كفاية للعاقل
The speech of Allah, the Most High, is uncreated. And it is narrated from Sufyan al-Thawri that he said: Whoever says the Qur'an is created is a disbeliever in Allah. The trustworthy narrators informed us with their chain from Ibn Umar from the Prophet, peace be upon him, that he said: There will come upon my Ummah a time when they say the Qur'an is created. So whoever among you lives and reaches them, do not dispute with them nor sit with them, for they are disbelievers in Allah the Magnificent, and they will not enter Paradise nor smell its fragrance. And Thabit al-Bunani said: When we heard this hadith, we knelt on our knees out of reverence for this hadith. And whoever suspends judgment and does not say that it is the speech of Allah, the Most High, is worse than the one who says the Qur'an is created. And the suspender who says 'I do not know whether the Qur'an is created or uncreated' is like the Christians who split into three sects: one sect said, 'We have seen from Jesus the giving of life to the dead, and giving life to the dead is the act of the deity, so we say he is God.' The second sect said, 'We have seen from him servitude, so we say he is a servant.' The third sect said, 'We have seen from him divinity and servitude, so we do not say he is a servant nor God.' And the suspender says something similar. Know that all the books that Allah, the Most High, sent down from the time of Adam, peace be upon him, to His prophets until the time of Muhammad, peace be upon him, are one hundred and four books, all of them are the speech of Allah, uncreated. And it is narrated in some reports from Ka'b al-Ahbar that he said: Allah, the Most High, sent down forty pages to Seth son of Adam, thirty pages to Idris, twenty pages to Ibrahim, and ten pages to Musa before the Torah, then He sent down the Torah to Musa, the Psalms to Dawud, the Gospel to Isa, and the Criterion to Muhammad, may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon them all. So all these books are the speech of Allah, the Most High, and His attribute, and it is uncreated. Whoever says one word of them is created is a disbeliever in Allah, called a Jahmi and a Mu'tazili, and there is no doubt about his disbelief, for he is an innovator. And this proof suffices for the intelligent person.
Source pages p. 12

Commentary
This passage is a doctrinal argument establishing that the Quran (and all revealed books) is the uncreated speech of Allah, and refuting those who claim it is created. The author begins by stating the core belief: 'The speech of Allah is uncreated.' He then cites a narration from Sufyan al-Thawri that whoever says the Quran is created is a disbeliever. He then provides a hadith from Ibn Umar that the Prophet foretold a time when people will say the Quran is created, and commands believers to avoid them, as they are disbelievers. Thabit al-Bunani's reaction of kneeling in reverence shows the gravity of this issue. Next, the author discusses the 'waqif' (one who suspends judgment) who neither affirms nor denies. He ranks the waqif as worse than the one who says it is created, because the waqif's hesitation implies doubt. He then draws an analogy with the Christian sects who erred about Jesus: one said he is God, one said he is a servant, and one suspended judgment (neither). The waqif among Muslims is likened to that third Christian group. The author then informs that all 104 divine books (from Adam to Muhammad) are uncreated speech of Allah. He lists the specific books and pages revealed to various prophets. The conclusion is that whoever says any word of these books is created is a disbeliever, labeled as Jahmi or Mu'tazili, and is an innovator without doubt. The proof is sufficient for the intelligent.

Key Terms
جهمي
معتزلي

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some may think that only the Quran is uncreated, while other scriptures are created.
Correction: The author explicitly states that all 104 books, including pages to earlier prophets, are the uncreated speech of Allah.

Checkpoint
According to the passage, how many total books/pages did Allah send down from Adam to Muhammad?
A) 100 books and 4 pages
B) 104 books
C) 100 books
D) 104 pages

Key Takeaway
All 104 divine books are the uncreated speech of Allah; denying this makes one a disbeliever and innovator.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
If someone you deeply respect or love told you that the Quran is a created thing, would you have the courage to declare their error as kufr, as this passage demands? What would that mean for your relationship?
?
This passage says the proof is sufficient for the intelligent. Are you willing to accept this truth with full certainty, or do you still harbor intellectual doubts that hold you back from complete submission? What are those doubts?
?
Do you treat the Torah, Gospel, and Psalms as mere human writings, or as the actual uncreated speech of Allah? How does your view of other scriptures affect your reverence for them and your understanding of Allah's attribute of speech?

Divine Attributes

God's Anger and Pleasure Are Real Attributes
Samarqandi defends God's anger and pleasure as literal, uncreated attributes.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand why God's anger and pleasure are real, uncreated attributes distinct from their created effects like Fire and Paradise.

Core Idea
God's anger and pleasure are real, uncreated attributes, not the Fire or Paradise.

This passage from Imam Samarqandi defends the orthodox Sunni position that God's attributes of anger and pleasure are literal and uncreated. The author warns against reducing these attributes to their created consequences and emphasizes the distinction between Creator and creature.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage and translation
Read the Arabic passage carefully, then its literal translation. Notice the author's emphatic tone and the clear prohibitions.
The passage uses 'know that' (اعلم) to signal an important point.
2
Identify the key claims and prohibitions
Find what the author affirms (Allah has anger and pleasure) and what he prohibits (saying anger is Fire or pleasure is Paradise; equating God's attributes to human ones).
The term 'innovator' (مبتدع) is used twice for those who hold wrong views.
3
Examine the argument and evidence
Trace the logical argument: human attributes are created, God's attributes are uncreated; anger and pleasure are His attributes, so uncreated. Then see the Quranic proofs that distinguish attribute from effect.
The verses about forgiveness and punishment show that God's pleasure/anger are greater than Paradise/Fire.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
وما قلنا انه ينبني له ان يعلم ان الله تعالى يغضب ويرضى ولا يقول ان غضب الله النار ورضاء الجنة فمن قال هذا فهو مبتدع ) واعلم ( ان الله تعالى غضبا ورضى وليس غضب الله ورضاء كفضينا ورضانا فمن قال هذا فهو مبتدع وعضنا ورضانا اذا دخل فينا غيرنا عن حالنا وغضب الله ورضاء لا ينيره عن حاله لان انفسنا وما يحى منامن خير وشرفهو مخلوق والله تعالى بجميع صفاته غير مخلوق وغضبه ورضاه صفته فليستا مخلوقتين وكل شيء يكون مخلوقا لا يكون صفة الخالق والنار تستوجب بغضب الله والجنة تستوجب برضى الله والدليل عليه قوله تعالى ورضوان من الله اكبر الاية واما فى غضبه فقوله تعالى ومن مقتل مؤمنا متعمدا فجزاؤه جهنم الآية وقوله تعالى عليهم دائرة السوء وغضب الله عليهم ولعنهم الآية وهذا كفاية للعاقل
We said that it is necessary for him to know that Allah Most High becomes angry and is pleased, and he should not say that God's anger is the Fire and His pleasure is Paradise. Whoever says this is an innovator. Know that Allah Most High has anger and pleasure, and God's anger and pleasure are not like our anger and pleasure. Whoever says this is an innovator. Our anger and pleasure, when something enters us that changes our state, [are created]; but God's anger and pleasure do not change His state. For our selves and what lives from us of good and honor are created, while Allah Most High with all His attributes is uncreated. His anger and pleasure are His attributes, so they are not created. Everything that is created cannot be an attribute of the Creator. The Fire is earned by God's anger, and Paradise is earned by God's pleasure. The evidence for it is His saying: 'And forgiveness from Allah is greater' – the verse. As for His anger, His saying: 'And whoever kills a believer intentionally, his recompense is Hell' – the verse, and His saying: 'Upon them is the evil circle, and Allah is angry with them and curses them' – the verse. This is sufficient for the intelligent person.
Source pages p. 18

Commentary
This passage is a theological refutation of those who misinterpret God's attributes of anger and pleasure. The author first states the correct belief: Allah gets angry and is pleased, but one must not say that His anger is the Fire itself or that His pleasure is Paradise itself. Such a statement is innovation. The key distinction is that God's anger and pleasure are real attributes, uncreated, unlike human anger and pleasure which are created emotions that change one's state. The author argues: human attributes are created because humans are creatures; God's attributes are inseparable from Him and are therefore uncreated. Then he demonstrates that the Fire is a consequence of God's anger, and Paradise a consequence of God's pleasure, not identical to them. He provides Quranic evidence: the verse 'And forgiveness from Allah is greater' implying the pleasure is greater than Paradise, and verses threatening punishment for murder and the disbelievers as proof of God's anger.

Key Terms
غضب
رضا
صفات
مخلوق
غير مخلوق

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some think that saying 'God's anger is the Fire' is a valid metaphorical interpretation that does not deny the attribute.
Correction: The author insists that such a statement is innovation and that the attributes are real and distinct from their created effects.

Checkpoint
What does the author say about one who claims that God's anger is literally the Fire?
A) He is a believer
B) He is an innovator
C) He is a disbeliever
D) He is excused

Key Takeaway
God's anger and pleasure are real, uncreated attributes distinct from their created rewards and punishments.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
If God's anger is real and uncreated, and the Fire is only its consequence, how seriously do you take the reality of His anger when you sin? Do you think of the punishment, or do you forget the One who is angry?
?
The pleasure of Allah is greater than Paradise itself. In your daily life, what are you truly seeking – the reward of Paradise or the pleasure of the Lord? How can you tell the difference?
?
The author says God's anger and pleasure do not change His state. Have you ever imagined God as being 'upset' or 'pleased' in a human way? How can you worship a God whose attributes are real but entirely other than yours?

Divine Attributes
beginner
4 min
God's Anger and Pleasure Are Uncreated Attributes
Samarqandi affirms God's anger and pleasure as uncreated attributes, refuting equating them with Fire and Paradise.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand that God's anger and pleasure are uncreated attributes distinct from created entities like Fire and Paradise, and that they do not imply change in God.

Core Idea
God's anger and pleasure are real attributes, not created things.

In Islamic theology, attributes of God are eternal and uncreated. However, some have mistakenly equated God's anger with the Fire and His pleasure with Paradise. In this passage, Imam al-Samarqandi clarifies the correct belief: God truly becomes angry and is pleased, but these attributes are not like our emotions nor are they created. He provides both rational and scriptural proofs to guard against innovation.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage
Read the Arabic passage and its translation carefully to get an overview.
2
Identify the main claim
Notice that Samarqandi starts by stating the correct belief and rejecting a false one.
The false belief is that God's anger is the Fire and His pleasure is Paradise.
3
Understand the distinction
Focus on the explanation that human anger changes us, but God's anger does not change Him.
Human emotions are caused by external factors, while God's attributes are inherent and unchanging.
4
Examine the evidence
Look at the Quranic verses cited as proof that anger and pleasure are attributes distinct from their consequences.
5
Reflect on the implication
Consider why equating attributes with created things leads to innovation (bid'ah).
Innovation is attributing createdness to God's attributes.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
المسئلة التاسعة والعشرون
وما قلنا انه ينبغي له ان يعلم ان الله تعالى يغضب ويرضى ولا يقول ان غضب الله النار ورضاء الجنة فمن قال هذا فهو مبتدع ) واعلم ( ان الله تعالى غضبا ورضى وليس غضب الله ورضاء كغضبنا ورضانا فمن قال هذا فهو مبتدع وغضبنا ورضانا اذا دخل فينا غيرنا عن حالنا وغضب الله ورضاء لا يغيره عن حاله لان انفسنا وما يحى من [؟] خير وشرف هو مخلوق والله تعالى بجميع صفاته غير مخلوق وغضبه ورضاه صفته فليستا مخلوقتين وكل شيء يكون مخلوقا لا يكون صفة الخالق والنار تستوجب بغضب الله والجنة تستوجب برضى الله والدليل عليه قوله تعالى ورضوان من الله اكبر الاية واما فى غضبه فقوله تعالى ومن يقتل مؤمنا متعمدا فجزاؤه جهنم الآية وقوله تعالى عليهم دائرة السوء وغضب الله عليهم ولعنهم الآية وهذا كفاية للمعاقل
Question twenty-nine: What we said that he should know that God Almighty becomes angry and is pleased, and he should not say that God's anger is the Fire and His pleasure is Paradise. Whoever says this is an innovator. Know that God Almighty has anger and pleasure, and God's anger and pleasure are not like our anger and pleasure. Whoever says this is an innovator. Our anger and pleasure occur when something enters us that changes our state, but God's anger and pleasure do not change His state. Because our souls and what [?] of good and honor are created, while God Almighty with all His attributes is uncreated. His anger and pleasure are His attributes, so they are not created. Everything that is created cannot be an attribute of the Creator. The Fire is deserved by God's anger, and Paradise is deserved by God's pleasure. The evidence for that is His saying: {And pleasure from God is greater} (Quran 9:72). As for His anger, His saying: {And whoever kills a believer intentionally, his reward is Hell} (Quran 4:93), and His saying: {Upon them is a cycle of evil, and God's wrath is upon them, and He has cursed them} (Quran 48:6). This is sufficient for the intelligent.
Source pages p. 18

Commentary
This passage is a doctrinal definition and refutation. Samarqandi begins by stating the issue: the believer must affirm that God has anger and pleasure, but must not say that these are identical to the Fire and Paradise. He declares that those who do so are innovators. He then clarifies that God's anger and pleasure are not like human anger and pleasure, because human emotions are changes caused by external factors, whereas God's attributes do not imply change. He supports this by noting that humans and their qualities are created, but God and His attributes are uncreated. Anger and pleasure are attributes of God, hence they are uncreated; anything created cannot be an attribute of the Creator. The Fire is a consequence of God's anger, and Paradise is a consequence of His pleasure, but they are not the attributes themselves. He provides Quranic evidence: the verse about 'pleasure from God' indicating it is something greater than Paradise, and verses about divine wrath and punishment. The passage concludes that this is sufficient for the rational person.

Key Terms
غضب الله
رضى الله
صفات
مبتدع

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some think that God's anger is literally the Fire and His pleasure is literally Paradise.
Correction: Samarqandi clarifies that God's anger and pleasure are uncreated attributes, not created entities. The Fire and Paradise are created consequences of these attributes.

Checkpoint
According to Samarqandi, why is it wrong to say God's anger is the Fire and His pleasure is Paradise?
A) Because fire and paradise are created, while God's attributes are uncreated.
B) Because God does not get angry or pleased.
C) Because anger and pleasure are only human emotions.
D) Because the Quran does not mention them.

Key Takeaway
God's anger and pleasure are uncreated attributes; they are not created entities like Fire or Paradise, nor are they subject to change.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
When you think about God's anger, do you imagine it as a mere abstract concept or as a real, personal attribute that demands your fear and reverence? How does this lesson's affirmation of God's anger as an uncreated attribute change the way you relate to Him in your moments of disobedience?
?
If you have ever considered God's pleasure to be simply 'good things happening' or 'Paradise,' how does this lesson correct that shallow understanding? What does it mean for your daily worship to know that God's pleasure is greater than Paradise itself—and that He is pleased with you is the ultimate goal?
?
Are you guilty of attributing human-like mood swings to God, thinking He 'gets angry' in the same way you do when you are upset? After learning that God's anger and pleasure do not change His state, how will you guard your heart against such anthropomorphism and instead stand in awe of His unchanging perfection?

Divine Attributes

Allah's Absolute Will and Justice
Samarqandi affirms Allah's absolute sovereignty and justice, warning against attributing injustice.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand that Allah's actions are inherently just, and that attributing injustice to Him is an act of disbelief.

Core Idea
Allah's actions are always just; human perception cannot judge them.

This passage from Imam Samarqandi firmly establishes a foundational principle of Sunni theology: whatever Allah does is just and wise. Our limited human understanding may perceive some events as evil, but in reality, they are part of Allah's perfect decree. The author warns that attributing injustice to Allah is an act of disbelief, and supports this with Qur'anic verses affirming Allah's absolute knowledge and power.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage and translation
Read the Arabic passage once to get the flow, then read the full English translation. Notice the author's emphatic tone.
2
Identify the central claim
Find the statement that Allah does whatever He wills and that His actions are always judgment and justice. Mark the phrase 'فما فعل الله تعالى فهو منه حكم وعدل'.
3
Note the warning
Observe the severe consequence: whoever describes Allah with injustice has disbelieved. Underline the sentence 'ومن وصف الله تعالى بالجور فقد كفر بالله'.
4
Reflect on the Quranic proofs
The author cites three verses to support the claim. Read each one and notice how they affirm Allah's knowledge, power, and control over all affairs.
5
Consider human limitation
The passage ends with the reminder that we may dislike what is good and love what is evil. Think about how this applies to your own understanding of divine decree.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
وما قلنا انه ينبغي له ان يعلم ان الله تعالى فعل ماشاء ويفعل مايشاء فهم الخلق اولم يفهم خيرا كان اوشراً فما فعل الله تعالى فهو منه حكم وعدل ولا يكون ذلك منه جورا ومن وصف الله تعالى بالجور فقد كفر بالله والله تعالى قادر خلقه على جميع وعالم بالاشياء لقوله تعالى ان الله بكل شيء عليم وقوله ان الله على كل شيء قدير والامور كلها بيد الله تعالى لقوله تعالى فاذا قضى امرا فانما يقول له كن فيكون ونحن ربما نكره شيأ وهو خير لنا وربما نحب شياً وهو شر لنا لقوله تعالى وعسى أن تكرهوا شيأ وهو خير لكم وعسى ان تحبوا شياً وهو شر لكم والله يعلم وانتم لا تعلمون وهذا كفاية للمعاقل
And what we said: it is fitting for him to know that Allah Almighty did whatever He willed and does whatever He wills, whether He creates them or does not create them, be it good or evil. So whatever Allah Almighty does, from Him it is judgment and justice, and there is no injustice from Him. And whoever describes Allah Almighty with injustice has disbelieved in Allah. And Allah Almighty is powerful over all His creation and Knower of all things, for He says: 'Indeed, Allah is of all things Knowing' (2:282) and 'Indeed, Allah is over all things Competent' (2:106). And all affairs are in the hand of Allah Almighty, for He says: 'So when He decrees a matter, He only says to it, "Be," and it is' (2:117). And we sometimes dislike something while it is good for us, and sometimes love something while it is evil for us, for He says: 'Perhaps you dislike something while it is good for you, and perhaps you love something while it is evil for you. And Allah knows, while you know not' (2:216). And this is sufficient for the intelligent.
Source pages p. 22

Commentary
This passage is a theological statement affirming Allah's absolute sovereignty and justice. The author asserts that Allah does whatever He wills, whether He creates or not, whether the outcome seems good or evil to us. All of Allah's actions are inherently judgment and justice; it is impossible for injustice to proceed from Him. Anyone who attributes injustice to Allah commits disbelief. The author supports this with Qur'anic proofs: Allah is all-knowing and all-powerful, and all affairs are subject to His command 'Be'. He then quotes verse 2:216 to remind that human perception is limited—we may dislike what is actually good for us and love what is evil. This underscores that Allah's actions are always wise and just, even if we do not understand them. The conclusion is that this teaching is sufficient for those with intellect.

Key Terms
فعل
مشيئة
جور
عدل
حكم
قدرة
علم

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some think that if evil occurs, it must be because Allah is unjust.
Correction: This passage clarifies that all actions of Allah are just by definition, and human perception of good/evil is not the measure.

Checkpoint
What does Samarqandi say about describing Allah with injustice (jūr)?
A) It is a major sin but not disbelief.
B) It is disbelief (kufr).
C) It is permissible if one repents.
D) It is a matter of interpretation.

Key Takeaway
Allah's actions are always just; we must trust His wisdom and never attribute injustice to Him.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
Think of a recent hardship in your life that felt unfair. After reading that 'we sometimes dislike something while it is good for us,' how will you train your heart to see Allah's justice even in that pain? What specific verse from this passage will you memorize to combat resentment when it arises?
?
The passage warns that describing Allah with injustice is disbelief. Have you ever, even inwardly, accused Allah of being unjust? What does that reveal about your understanding of His wisdom? Write down one area where you struggle to submit to His decree and commit to saying 'Allah is just' every time doubt enters your mind for the next week.
?
Allah says, 'And Allah knows, while you know not.' Is there a command of Allah that you have hesitated to follow because it seemed 'unfair' or 'too hard'? Based on this lesson, what is the only correct response? Will you act on that response today?

Divine Attributes
beginner
5 min
The Quran is Uncreated and Truly Present in Recitation and Writing
Samarqandi defines the Quran as truly uncreated speech and proves it via Jibril's revelation.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand that the Quran is the uncreated speech of Allah, truly present in our recitation and writing, as proven by the chain from Jibril.

Core Idea
The Quran we recite is truly Allah's speech, not a metaphor.

Samarqandi begins by establishing that the Quran is the uncreated speech of Allah, and that our recitation and the writing of children are truly the Quran, not mere representations. He then provides a key proof: Jibril heard the Quran from Allah in reality and revealed it to Muhammad in reality, so it is truly Allah's speech. Any claim of metaphorical revelation would split the Quran into two, which is impossible.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage
Read the Arabic passage once without trying to analyse it. Then read the translation. Let the author's own rhythm come through before you interpret.
Notice the direct and assertive tone.
2
Identify the main claim
The passage affirms that the Quran is uncreated and that our recitation and children's writing are truly the Quran, not mere metaphors.
3
Examine the proof from Jibril
Trace the argument: Did Jibril hear the Quran in reality or metaphorically? If in reality, then it is revealed in reality, so the Quran in mushafs is truly Allah's speech.
This is a logical chain based on the transmission of revelation.
4
Understand the refutation
If someone claims part was revealed metaphorically, that would create two Qurans, which is impossible.
5
Reflect on the importance
Why does this matter for our belief? It establishes that the Quran we interact with is genuinely the speech of Allah.
This connects to the doctrine of the Quran being uncreated.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
ونحن نقرأ القرآن بالحقيقة وفينا القرآن وما يكتب الصبيان في الالواح هو قرأن ) واعلم ( ان القرآن كلام الله غير مخلوق ومن أنكر وقال ان مافي المصاحف ليس يقرأن بالحقيقة فقل له ان جبريل عليه السلام سمع هذا القرآن بالحقيقة او بالمجاز فان كان سمعه بالحقيقة وانزل على محمد صلى الله عليه وسلم بالمجاز فقد كتم بالحقيقة وانه انزل على محمد صلى الله عليه وسلم بالحقيقة فلم ينكر انه كلام الله تعالى فان قال المخالف بعض من القرآن انزل بالحقيقة وبعضه انزل بالمجاز فقد صار القرأن قرأنين وهذا محال ومن انكر التنزيل فهو كافر بالله تعالى فان قال ليس فى الدنيا قرأن ولا فى المصاحف والكراريس فقل له اين قول الله تعالى تبارك الذى نزل الفرقان على عبده وقوله انا نحن نزلنا الذكر وقوله طه ما انزلنا عليك القرآن لتشقى وقوله هم تنزيل الكتاب وقوله لو انزلنا هذا القرأن على جبل وقوله وانه لتنزيل رب العالين ومن انكر وقال ليس مافي المصاحف قرأنا فقد انكر الايات كلها لان اسم الكتاب لا يقع الا على شيء يكون فيه مكتوبا وقد قال الله تعالى الم ذلك الكتاب لا ريب فيه يعنى لاشك فيه فالله تعالى امر بقراءة القرآن فقال فاقرؤا ماتيسر من القرآن والله تعالى امر بالاستماع لقوله تعالى واذا قرئ القرآن فاستمعوا له وانصتوا الاية وما تقرأ انت ونحن نسمع منك كلام الله تعالى بالحقيقة والدليل قوله تعالى يسمعون كلام الله ثم يحرفون من بعد ما عقلوه الآية وقوله حتى يسمع كلام الله فالله تعالى من على النبي صلى الله تعالى عليه وسلم وقال ولقد اتيناك سبعا من المثانى والقرآن العظيم فاذا لم يكن ما فى المصاحف فاتحة الكتاب. ولاما في الكراريس فبأي شيء من عليه قالله تعالى نهي عن مس المصاحف الإيجال الطهارة لقوله تعالى لا يمسه الا المطهرون تنزيل من رب العالمين وقوله انه لقرآن كريم الاية فلولم يكن ما في المصاحف قرآنا ما نهى عن مسها
We recite the Quran in reality, and within us is the Quran, and what the children write on the tablets is the Quran. Know that the Quran is the speech of Allah, uncreated. Whoever denies and says that what is in the mushafs is not recited in reality, say to him: Did Jibril hear this Quran in reality or metaphorically? If he heard it in reality and then revealed it to Muhammad (SAW) metaphorically, then he concealed it in reality; and it was revealed to Muhammad (SAW) in reality, so why do you deny that it is the speech of Allah? If the opponent says: Some of the Quran was revealed in reality and some was revealed metaphorically, then the Quran would become two Qurans, and this is impossible. Whoever denies revelation is a disbeliever in Allah. If he says: There is no Quran in the world, nor in the mushafs and books, then say to him: Where is the saying of Allah: 'Blessed is He who sent down the Criterion upon His servant' and His saying: 'Indeed, We sent down the Reminder' and His saying: 'Ta Ha, We have not sent down the Quran upon you to cause you distress' and His saying: 'It is the revelation of the Book' and His saying: 'If We had sent down this Quran upon a mountain' and His saying: 'And indeed, it is the revelation of the Lord of the worlds'? Whoever denies and says: What is in the mushafs is not Quran has denied all these verses, because the name 'Book' only applies to something that is written in it. And Allah said: 'Alif Lam Mim, that is the Book, no doubt in it', meaning no doubt in it. Allah commanded the recitation of the Quran: 'So recite what is easy of the Quran'. And Allah commanded listening: 'And when the Quran is recited, listen to it and be silent'. And you recite and we hear from you the speech of Allah in reality, as evidenced by His saying: 'They hear the speech of Allah then distort it after they had understood it' and His saying: 'Until they hear the speech of Allah'. Allah bestowed upon the Prophet (SAW) and said: 'And We have given you seven of the oft-repeated verses and the great Quran'. So if what is in the mushafs is not the Opening of the Book, nor what is in the books, then with what did He bestow upon him? Allah forbids touching the mushafs except with purity: 'None touch it except the purified, a revelation from the Lord of the worlds'. And His saying: 'Indeed, it is a noble Quran'. If what is in the mushafs were not Quran, He would not have forbidden touching them.
Source pages p. 23

Commentary
This passage is a definition and proof that the Quran is the uncreated speech of Allah, truly present in recitation and writing. The author first states that our recitation and children's writing are truly the Quran. Then he presents a proof from the chain of transmission: Jibril heard the Quran from Allah in reality and revealed it to Muhammad in reality, so it is truly Allah's speech. The claim that part was revealed metaphorically would split the Quran into two, which is impossible. Denying revelation is kufr. Next, he refutes the denial of the Quran's existence in the world by citing numerous verses that speak of revelation. He argues that the name 'Book' applies to written texts, and Allah commands recitation and listening, proving that what is recited is truly His speech. Finally, he uses the prohibition of touching the mushaf except by the purified as evidence that the mushaf contains the Quran. The overall argument is a defense of the reality of the Quran as the uncreated speech of Allah present in the world.

Key Terms
قرآن
كلام الله
غير مخلوق
حقيقة
مجاز
مصحف

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: The Quran in the mushaf is only a metaphor or representation of the eternal speech.
Correction: The author argues that the recited and written Quran is truly the speech of Allah, not a metaphor.

Checkpoint
How does the author refute the claim that some of the Quran was revealed metaphorically?
A) By citing the verse about the mountain
B) By arguing that it would make two Qurans
C) By saying it is impossible
D) By saying the opponent is a disbeliever

Key Takeaway
The Quran we recite is truly the uncreated speech of Allah, proven by the reality of Jibril's revelation.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
When you recite the Quran, do you truly believe you are uttering the very words of Allah, uncreated and eternal? Or do you sometimes recite it absentmindedly, as if it were ordinary speech? How will you remind yourself of this reality in your next recitation?
?
The Quran is uncreated and truly present in your heart and in the mushaf. Is your relationship with the Quran one of reverence and obedience? Do you feel the weight of carrying Allah's eternal speech? If not, what step will you take right now to change that?
?
If someone claimed that the Quran you recite is only a metaphor, would you be able to refute them using the argument from this lesson? Are you prepared to defend the honor of Allah's speech? Memorize the proof and commit to teaching it to one person.

beginner
5 min
Refuting Denial of Revelation and the Quran's Presence in Mushafs
Samarqandi refutes denial by citing revelation verses and the definition of 'Book'.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand that denying the Quran's presence in the world or in mushafs contradicts numerous Quranic verses and the definition of 'Book'.

Core Idea
The Quran is not only in heaven; it is present in the mushafs as the revealed Book.

Samarqandi now confronts the extreme denial: that there is no Quran in the world or in the mushafs. He cites a series of verses that speak of revelation and implies that denying the Quran's presence in mushafs is to deny all these verses. He also argues that the name 'Book' (kitab) applies only to something written, and Allah calls the Quran a Book.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage
Read the Arabic and translation carefully. Note the list of verses and the logical argument.
The passage contains a chain of citations; pay attention to the flow.
2
List the cited verses
Identify the six verses that speak of revelation. What do they all have in common?
They all affirm that Allah sent down the Quran.
3
Understand the argument from 'Book'
The author argues that the name 'Book' implies writing. Since Allah calls the Quran 'the Book', it must exist in written form.
4
Reflect on the refutation
How does this refute the claim that there is no Quran in the mushafs?
Denying the Quran in mushafs means denying the verses that call it a revealed Book.

?

?
If you were to deny even one verse of the Quran, you would be in danger of kufr. Are there any verses you find difficult to accept? How do you reconcile that with your faith?
?
The passage shows that denying the Quran in the mushaf is to deny all revelation. Do you ever act as if the Quran is not present in your life by ignoring its commands? What would it mean for you to truly acknowledge the Quran as present?

Evidence from Commands to Recite and Listen and the Prohibition of Touching
Samarqandi uses commands to recite/listen and prohibition of touching to prove the Quran's reality.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand that the commands to recite and listen to the Quran, and the prohibition of touching the mushaf without purity, prove that the recited and written Quran is truly Allah's speech.

Core Idea
Allah commands us to recite and listen to His speech in reality, and forbids touching the mushaf without purity.

Samarqandi provides further evidence: Allah commands us to recite the Quran, and to listen to it when recited. He also quotes verses that mention hearing the speech of Allah. Then he cites the prohibition of touching the mushaf except by the purified, arguing that if the mushaf did not contain the Quran, there would be no such prohibition. This confirms that the Quran is truly present in the mushaf.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage
Read the Arabic and translation. Notice the two distinct proofs.
2
Identify the commands
What does Allah command regarding the Quran? Recitation and listening.
3
Understand the argument from hearing
The author argues that we hear the speech of Allah in reality when the Quran is recited, citing verses that mention hearing Allah's speech.
4
Grasp the evidence from the prohibition
Allah forbids touching the mushaf without purity. If the mushaf did not contain the Quran, this prohibition would be pointless.
5
Summarize the overall proof
Together, these two proofs confirm that the Quran in recitation and writing is truly Allah's speech.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
ونحن نقرأ القرآن بالحقيقة وفينا القرآن وما يكتب الصبيان في الالواح هو قرأن ) واعلم ( ان القرآن كلام الله غير مخلوق ومن أنكر وقال ان مافي المصاحف ليس يقرأن بالحقيقة فقل له ان جبريل عليه السلام سمع هذا القرآن بالحقيقة او بالمجاز فان كان سمعه بالحقيقة وانزل على محمد صلى الله عليه وسلم بالمجاز فقد كتم بالحقيقة وانه انزل على محمد صلى الله عليه وسلم بالحقيقة فلم ينكر انه كلام الله تعالى فان قال المخالف بعض من القرآن انزل بالحقيقة وبعضه انزل بالمجاز فقد صار القرأن قرأنين وهذا محال ومن انكر التنزيل فهو كافر بالله تعالى فان قال ليس فى الدنيا قرأن ولا فى المصاحف والكراريس فقل له اين قول الله تعالى تبارك الذى نزل الفرقان على عبده وقوله انا نحن نزلنا الذكر وقوله طه ما انزلنا عليك القرآن لتشقى وقوله هم تنزيل الكتاب وقوله لو انزلنا هذا القرأن على جبل وقوله وانه لتنزيل رب العالين ومن انكر وقال ليس مافي المصاحف قرأنا فقد انكر الايات كلها لان اسم الكتاب لا يقع الا على شيء يكون فيه مكتوبا وقد قال الله تعالى الم ذلك الكتاب لا ريب فيه يعنى لاشك فيه فالله تعالى امر بقراءة القرآن فقال فاقرؤا ماتيسر من القرآن والله تعالى امر بالاستماع لقوله تعالى واذا قرئ القرآن فاستمعوا له وانصتوا الاية وما تقرأ انت ونحن نسمع منك كلام الله تعالى بالحقيقة والدليل قوله تعالى يسمعون كلام الله ثم يحرفون من بعد ما عقلوه الآية وقوله حتى يسمع كلام الله فالله تعالى من على النبي صلى الله تعالى عليه وسلم وقال ولقد اتيناك سبعا من المثانى والقرآن العظيم فاذا لم يكن ما فى المصاحف فاتحة الكتاب. ولاما في الكراريس فبأي شيء من عليه قالله تعالى نهي عن مس المصاحف الإيجال الطهارة لقوله تعالى لا يمسه الا المطهرون تنزيل من رب العالمين وقوله انه لقرآن كريم الاية فلولم يكن ما في المصاحف قرآنا ما نهى عن مسها

Misconception & Correction

Checkpoint
What evidence does the author give from the prohibition of touching the mushaf?
A) It shows the mushaf is pure
B) It proves the mushaf contains the Quran

Key Takeaway
The commands to recite and listen, and the prohibition of touching without purity, confirm that the Quran in recitation and writing is truly Allah's speech.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
When you recite the Quran, do you truly believe you are uttering Allah's actual speech, or do you treat it as mere words? How would your recitation change if you were fully convinced that every letter is from Him?
?
This passage argues that the prohibition of touching the mushaf without purity confirms it is Allah's speech. Are you careful about purity when handling the Quran? What does your level of care reveal about your belief in the Quran's divine origin?
?
If a non-Muslim asked you why Muslims honor the Quran physically, could you give the arguments from this passage? If not, what does that say about your own understanding and conviction?

Allah's Speech Without Created Attributes
Samarqandi states that Allah spoke the Quran without created attributes and answers questions about it.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand that Allah's attribute of speech is eternal and free from any created characteristics like letters, sounds, or time.

Core Idea
Allah's speech is without letters, sound, or time.

Imam al-Samarqandi begins by establishing a foundational doctrine: Allah's speech is not like human speech. The Quran is the eternal word of Allah, not composed of letters or sounds, and not subject to time or sequence. This lesson clarifies that divine speech transcends created attributes and answers common questions about its nature.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage and translation
Read the Arabic passage once without analysis, then the translation. Notice the list of negations and the Q&A format.
The passage is dense; let the author's words stand first.
2
Identify what Allah's speech is NOT
The passage explicitly states Allah said the Quran without spelling, letter, teaching, melody, sound, and time. Understand each term as a negation of created attributes.
These negations (tanzih) are core to the Ash'ari doctrine.
3
Examine the Q&A responses
The questions about 'when', 'how', 'where', etc., are all answered with 'bila' (without). This reinforces that Allah's speech is not bound by temporal or spatial categories.
This method of negation is common in kalam to affirm transcendence.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
(واعلم) أن الله تعالى قال هذا القرآن بلا هجاء ولا حرف وبلا تعليم بعد تعليم وبلا نغمة بعد نغمة وبلا صوت بعد صوت وبلا وقت بعد وقت فالله تعالى قال هذا القرآن بلا هجاء ولا حرف ولا صوت كما قلنا وسمع جبريل عليه السلام من الله تعالى مثل ذلك وقرأ جبريل على محمد صلى الله عليه وسلم بحرف وصوت ونحن نقرأ بصوت وحرف ونكتب بحرف وليس فرق بين الذي قال الله تعالى وبين الذي سمعه جبريل من الله تعالى وبين الذي قرأ جبريل على محمد صلى الله عليه وسلم وبين الذي قرأ محمد على خلق الله تعالى وما نقرأ بيننا لأن كله كلام الله غير مخلوق وان قرأ ونكتب في المصاحف فهو قرآن لا نزيد فيه حرفا ولا ننقص منه والورق والكاغد والمداد والقلم مخلوق والمكتوب كلام الله تعالى غير مخلوق بالحقيقة ومن قال القرآن مخلوق فهو كافر بالله فان قال المخالف هل قال الله تعالى الكلام فقل نعم فان قال متى فقل بلا متى فان قال كيف فقل بلا كيف فان قال أين فقل بلا أين فان قال كم فقل بلا كم فان قال حفظا أو رفعا فقل لا حفظا ولا رفعا فان قال بصوت أو بغير صوت فقل بلا صوت ولا حرف
Know that Allah said this Quran without spelling and without letter, without teaching after teaching, without melody after melody, without sound after sound, and without time after time. Allah said this Quran without spelling, letter, or sound as we have said. Jibril heard from Allah the like of that, and Jibril recited to Muhammad (SAW) with letter and sound, and we recite with sound and letter and write with letter. There is no difference between what Allah said and what Jibril heard from Allah, and what Jibril recited to Muhammad and what Muhammad recited to the people, and what we recite among ourselves, because all of it is the speech of Allah, uncreated. And if one recites and we write in the mushafs, it is the Quran; we do not add a letter to it nor subtract from it. The paper, the ink, and the pen are created, but the written is the speech of Allah, uncreated in reality. Whoever says the Quran is created is a disbeliever in Allah. If the opponent says: Did Allah say the speech? Say: Yes. If he says: When? Say: Without when. If he says: How? Say: Without how. If he says: Where? Say: Without where. If he says: How much? Say: Without how much. If he says: Preserved or raised? Say: Neither preserved nor raised. If he says: With sound or without sound? Say: Without sound and without letter.
Source pages p. 23

Commentary
This passage is a theological exposition on the nature of Allah's attribute of speech. The author clarifies that Allah spoke the Quran without the created elements of spelling, letters, sounds, melody, or time. Jibril heard it from Allah in this manner, but when conveying it to the Prophet, he used letters and sounds. Our recitation and writing involve these created means, but the essence of the speech remains uncreated. The physical mediums (paper, ink, pen) are created, but the written text is truly the uncreated speech. Then the author answers a series of questions about the attribute of speech, negating any temporal, spatial, or quantitative limitations, and affirming that Allah's speech is without sound or letter. This is a classic Ash'ari position distinguishing the eternal attribute from its temporal expressions.

Key Terms
هجاء
حرف
نغمة
صوت
وقت
بلا

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some think God's speech is like human speech, composed of letters and sounds.
Correction: The passage explicitly negates letters, sounds, and time, showing Allah's speech transcends creation.

Checkpoint
According to this passage, how did Allah speak the Quran?
A) With letters and sounds
B) With spelling and teaching
C) Without letters, sounds, or time
D) In a loud voice

Key Takeaway
Allah's speech is eternal and free from all created attributes such as letters, sounds, and time.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
When you recite the Quran with your voice and letters, do you forget that its origin is eternal and beyond sound? What would change in your heart if you truly believed every letter you utter originates from a speech that has no sound?
?
You have no answer for 'When?' regarding Allah's speech. Yet you live your entire life in time. How does this eternal word demand that you reorient your priorities away from what is temporary?
?
If Allah's speech came without teaching after teaching, then you already have everything you need for guidance. What keeps you from submitting to its decisive verses instead of arguing over doubtful matters?

The Uncreated Quran: Transmission and Expression
Samarqandi describes the transmission chain and affirms the Quran is uncreated despite created means.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand how the uncreated Quran relates to its recitation and writing, and why considering the Quran created is disbelief.

Core Idea
The Quran is uncreated; its recitation and writing are created expressions.

Samarqandi now explains the chain of transmission: Allah spoke the eternal speech, Jibril heard it without letters or sounds, then recited it to the Prophet with letters and sounds, and we recite it likewise. Despite these created mediums, the speech itself remains uncreated. He also addresses the written mushaf and the severe consequence of denying this doctrine.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage about transmission
Read the portion where Jibril hears from Allah, recites to the Prophet, and we recite. Notice that there is no difference in essence between what Allah said and what we recite.
The chain emphasizes continuity despite different modes.
2
Understand the distinction between speech and expression
The recitation and writing involve created means (letters, sounds, paper, ink), but the speech itself is uncreated. The author explicitly says the paper, ink, pen are created, but the written is uncreated.
This resolves the apparent contradiction of an uncreated Quran in created form.
3
Reflect on the ruling
The author declares that whoever says the Quran is created is a disbeliever. This shows the gravity of the doctrine.
This is a strong statement typical of early Sunni creeds.

Source Text

And if one recites and we write in the mushafs, it is the Quran; we do not add a letter to it nor subtract from it. The paper, the ink, and the pen are created, but the written is the speech of Allah, uncreated in reality. Whoever says the Quran is created is a disbeliever in Allah. If the opponent says: Did Allah say the speech? Say: Yes. If he says: When? Say: Without when. If he says: How? Say: Without how. If he says: Where? Say: Without where. If he says: How much? Say: Without how much. If he says: Preserved or raised? Say: Neither preserved nor raised. If he says: With sound or without sound? Say: Without sound and without letter.

Refuting the Objection that Letters are Created
Samarqandi refutes the claim that written letters are created because they can be varied.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand why varying the length of written letters does not imply that the Quranic speech is created.

Core Idea
Variation in writing does not affect the uncreated nature of the Quran.

In this passage, Imam Samarqandi addresses a specific objection: if letters can be lengthened or shortened by the writer, does that make them created? He responds with a clear analogy from recitation, showing that variation is a property of the act, not the essence. This lesson will clarify the distinction between the physical representation and the divine speech.

Guided Path
1
Read the objection carefully
Identify the opponent's claim: written letters are created because the writer can control their length.
This is the starting point of the argument.
2
Understand the initial response
Notice how Samarqandi responds with a question: does varying the length remove the essential property of being a letter?
The term 'harfiyyah' (letterness) is key.
3
Reflect on the analogy with recitation
Samarqandi compares writing to recitation: one can recite long or short, but that does not describe the Quran itself as long or short.
The analogy shows that variation applies to the act, not the essence.
4
Consider the conclusion
The author firmly states that anyone who describes the Quran contrary to this is an innovator (mubtadi').
This underscores the seriousness of the doctrine.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
فإن قال المخالف: المكتوبات والحروف مخلوقة لأني أكتب، إن شئت طولت وإن شئت قصرت. فقل له: إن شئت تطويل الحروف أو تقصيرها، فهل يرتفع عنها اسم الحرفية؟ وإذا كان الناس يقولون: فلان طويل القراءة، فلان قصير القراءة، هل يجوز أن يقولوا: القرآن طويل أو قصير؟ والذي يطول القراءة ويخفف ويقصر فكله كلام الله تعالى، ليس فيه فرق. وكذلك من طول كتابته بالحروف أو قصر. ومن قال في القرآن شيء غير ما وصفنا فهو مبتدع. وهذا كفاية للعاقل.
If the opponent says: The written things and letters are created because when I write, if I want I lengthen and if I want I shorten. Say to him: If you lengthen the letters or shorten them, does the name 'letterness' (harfiyyah) cease from them? And if people say: So-and-so has a long recitation, so-and-so has a short recitation, is it permissible for them to say: The Quran is long or short? The one who prolongs the recitation, or lightens it, or shortens it, all of it is the speech of Allah, there is no difference in it. Similarly, whoever lengthens his writing of the letters or shortens. And whoever says about the Quran something other than what we have described is an innovator. And this is sufficient for the intelligent.
Source pages p. 23

Commentary
This passage is an objection-and-answer. The opponent argues that written letters are created because their size can be varied (lengthened or shortened). The author responds that varying the size does not remove the essential property of being a letter (harfiyyah). He uses an analogy: recitation can be long or short, but the Quran itself is not described as long or short; the variation pertains to the recitation, not the essence. Similarly, the physical writing is variable, but the Quranic speech remains uncreated. Those who describe the Quran in a manner contrary to this are innovators. The argument concludes that this explanation is sufficient for a reasonable person.

Key Terms
مكتوبات
حروف
مخلوقة
حرفية
مبتدع

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: The physical written letters are created because their size can be changed.
Correction: The variation in writing pertains to the physical representation, not the essence of the divine speech which is uncreated.

Checkpoint
What is the opponent's objection in this passage?
A) That letters are created because they can be lengthened or shortened
B) That recitation is created
C) That the Quran is created because it is written
D) That the Quran is long

Key Takeaway
The uncreated Quran remains unchanged despite variations in its written or recited form.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
When you pick up a Mushaf, do you ever forget that the physical book is not the Quran itself but a representation? How would your attitude change if you consistently distinguished between the created pages and the uncreated speech?
?
You naturally vary your recitation speed and tone. Do you ever worry that your imperfect recitation somehow diminishes the Quran? The passage says all recitation—long, short, light, or heavy—is still the speech of Allah. Does that liberate you from performance anxiety, or does it make you more careful how you recite?
?
If someone from a different madhab argued that the Quran is created because it is written in ink, would your heart tremble or would you confidently repeat the proof from this passage? What is the state of your certainty about the uncreatedness of Allah's speech?

Allah's Absolute Power over the Preserved Tablet
Samarqandi discusses the Preserved Tablet and Allah's power to change fates.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand that Allah has absolute power over the Preserved Tablet and can change a person's recorded fate based on their actions and final state.

Core Idea
Your final recorded destiny can change—Allah's power over the Tablet is absolute.

In this passage, Imam Samarqandi uses several examples from the Preserved Tablet to show that a person's recorded fate is not fixed. He illustrates how individuals like the magicians of Pharaoh, Abu Bakr, and Umar had their status changed based on their actions. This challenges a common misconception about predestination and emphasizes the importance of one's final deeds.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage carefully
Read the Arabic passage once without analyzing. Then read the translation. Notice the examples of people whose names were changed in the Tablet.
Pay attention to the pattern: each example shows a change from believer to disbeliever or vice versa.
2
Identify the main claim
The passage argues that Allah can erase and establish whatever He wills in the Preserved Tablet. Find the verse reference and the hadith that support this.
The key is the phrase 'yamuḥū allāhu mā yashā'u wa yuthbitu'.
3
Reflect on the implications
Consider how this teaching affects our understanding of divine decree and human free will. Does it mean predestination is conditional?
The passage does not deny predestination but affirms Allah's power to change based on deeds.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
بن آدم كان مؤمنا في اللوح فلما قتل اخاء ولم يرض بحكم الله محى اسمه من المؤمنين وكتب كافيا وسحرة فرغون ما داموا يسخرون كانت أسماؤهم فى اللوح من السحرة والكفرة فلما آمنوا وسجدوا كتبوا من المؤمنين وابو بكر وعمر ماداما يعبد ان الصنم كان اسمهما فى اللوح من الكافرين فلما اسلما كتب اسمهما في اللوح من المؤمنين وكذلك بلعم بن باعورا وقارون وثعلبة والله تعالى قادر في جميع الاحوال فعل ماشاء ويفعل ما يشاء يمحو الله ما يشاء ويثبت وعنده ام الكتاب ويشقى السعيد ويسعد الشقى ويصير الكافر مؤمنا ويصير المؤمن كافرا وقال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم يولد الانسان كافرا ويعيش كافرا ويموت مؤمنا وفي هذا اخبار كثيرة ولكن اقتصرنا وفى هذا كفاية للعاقل والله تعالى اعلم
The son of Adam was a believer in the Tablet. When he killed his brother and was not pleased with Allah's decree, Allah erased his name from the believers and wrote him as a disbeliever. The magicians of Pharaoh, as long as they were practicing magic, their names were in the Tablet among the magicians and disbelievers. When they believed and prostrated, they were written among the believers. Abu Bakr and Umar, as long as they were worshipping the idol, their names were in the Tablet among the disbelievers. When they embraced Islam, their names were written in the Tablet among the believers. Likewise Balaam bin Ba'ura, Qarun, and Tha'laba. Allah Almighty is powerful in all circumstances; He does what He wills and will do what He wills. He erases what He wills and establishes, and with Him is the Mother of the Book. He makes the fortunate wretched and the wretched fortunate; He makes the disbeliever a believer and makes the believer a disbeliever. The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: “A person is born a disbeliever, lives a disbeliever, and dies a believer.” There are many reports about this, but we have limited ourselves. In this is sufficient for the intelligent. And Allah knows best.
Source pages p. 36

Commentary
This passage presents a theological argument using examples from the Preserved Tablet (al-lawh al-mahfuz). The author demonstrates Allah's absolute power to change a person's recorded fate based on their actions. He lists several examples: Cain (Ibn Adam) who was initially a believer but became a disbeliever after murder; the magicians of Pharaoh who were disbelievers but became believers after their conversion; Abu Bakr and Umar who were disbelievers before Islam; and other figures like Balaam, Qarun, and Tha'laba. The author then quotes a hadith stating that a person may begin and end life as a disbeliever but die a believer, emphasizing that the final state is not fixed. The purpose is to affirm Allah's sovereignty over destinies and the importance of one's final actions.

Key Terms
اللوح (al-lawh)
محو (erase)
إثبات (establish)
أم الكتاب (Umm al-Kitab)
السعيد (the fortunate)
الشقي (the wretched)

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some people think that the Preserved Tablet fixes a person's final destiny irrevocably, so actions have no effect.
Correction: The passage shows that Allah can erase and rewrite names in the Tablet based on a person's actions and final state. Predestination does not negate the effect of deeds; rather, Allah's knowledge encompasses all, and He changes as He wills.

Checkpoint
According to the passage, what happened to Abu Bakr and Umar in the Preserved Tablet before they embraced Islam?
A) They were written as believers.
B) They were written as disbelievers.
C) They were not mentioned.
D) They were written as prophets.

Key Takeaway
Allah's power over the Preserved Tablet is absolute: He can change anyone's recorded destiny based on their final actions and faith.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
If your name in the Preserved Tablet can change based on your final actions, what does that mean for the sins you are currently attached to? Are you willing to risk that your current state might be recorded as 'disbeliever' if you die on it?
?
The magicians of Pharaoh changed from disbelievers to believers by a single act of prostration. Is there any act you are holding back from Allah out of fear or pride that could rewrite your destiny?
?
Abu Bakr and Umar were once idol worshippers. Their names changed. Do you believe that no matter how far you have strayed, Allah can still flip your record? What would it take for you to genuinely turn to Him today?
